REFERENCE:
The main bibliographic details of this citation (authors, title, journal, volume/issue/pages, and DOI) appear to correspond to a real and well-known paper by Collier et al. published in Nature in 2021 regarding B.1.1.7 variant sensitivity to vaccine-elicited antibodies. However, the PubMed identifiers are problematic:

1. **PMID 26083749** — This PMID falls in the range of publications from ~2015–2016 and does **not** correspond to this 2021 paper. The actual PMID for this paper should be in the 33 million range (e.g., ~33731933). This PMID is incorrectly attributed.

2. **PMCID PMC7616967** — Similarly, this PMCID does not appear to correspond to the correct PubMed Central record for this 2021 paper.

The rest of the citation (authors, title, journal name, year, volume 593, issue 7857, pages 136–141, and DOI 10.1038/s41586-021-03412-7) all align with the authentic publication.
authors: The author list is extensive and includes named individuals and consortia (CITIID-NIHR BioResource COVID-19 Collaboration, COVID-19 Genomics UK (COG-UK) Consortium). This matches the known real paper by Collier et al. (2021). The authors appear authentic.
title: The title 'Sensitivity of SARS-CoV-2 B.1.1.7 to mRNA vaccine-elicited antibodies' matches the real published paper. Authentic.
journal_name: The journal name is 'Nature', which is a legitimate, well-known, high-impact scientific journal published by Springer Nature. Authentic.
journal_volume: Volume 593 for Nature in 2021 is consistent with the journal's volume numbering for that period and this specific paper.
journal_issue: The journal is 'Nature', Volume 593, Issue 7857, pages 136–141, May 2021. These details are consistent with the real publication of this paper in Nature.
journal_page: Pages 136-141 in Volume 593, Issue 7857 are consistent with the real publication. Authentic.
elocator: The elocator is 10.1038/s41586-021-03412-7, which is the same as the DOI. This is a standard Nature journal locator and is consistent.
publication_date: The publication date of May 2021 is consistent with when this paper was published in Nature. Authentic.
doi: The DOI 10.1038/s41586-021-03412-7 follows the standard Nature/doi.org format and corresponds to the known published paper. This is an authentic, verifiable DOI.
pmcid: PMCID PMC7616967 does not correspond to the correct PubMed Central record for this paper. This PMCID is likely from an entirely different article and is incorrectly attributed here. Inauthentic.
pmid: PMID 26083749 is in the range of publications from approximately 2015–2016 and does not correspond to this 2021 paper. The correct PMID for this paper is in the ~33 million range. This identifier is incorrectly attributed. Inauthentic.
